strategy: The whole fight is his -90% Tower Shield. He casts it on himself on Round 1 and re-casts it 3 rounds after it comes off, so you never hit into it directly.
Do not use Pierce, Shatter, or Steal Ward to remove the shield — those trigger his "That's not funny!" cheat instead of working.

Exploit the fizzle cheat: when any wizard fizzles, he pushes all positive charms and wards to a random wizard — including his own Tower Shield. Deliberately fizzle a low-accuracy spell to strip the shield off him for free.

If you'd rather break it with damage, use an All-Enemies (AoE) spell — he only re-casts the shield once. A single-target damage spell makes him re-cast it twice, so never chip at him with single hits.

Stack your blades while the shield is up, then break it (fizzle-strip or AoE) and hit the same or next round before the 3-round timer restores it. Hit with Storm — he takes +30% from it and resists nearly everything else; Myth is worst (71% resist).
Keep charms and shields off yourself when you can — he occasionally Steal Charms a blade or Steal Wards a shield right off you.
cheatNotes: He shields himself for -90% almost the whole fight, so raw hits do almost nothing until the shield is gone. The 3-round re-cast timer is your attack window.
The obvious shield-strippers backfire here. Casting Pierce, Shatter, or a Steal Ward on his shield sets off the "That's not funny!" punishment instead of removing it.
Breaking the shield with an AoE (or one of the forbidden strippers) is the cheaper option — he only puts it back once.
Breaking the shield with a single-target damage spell is the worst option — he immediately re-shields twice, doubling your problem.
If you walk in with a blade or shield already up, he may just steal it. Set up the turn you plan to hit rather than banking buffs early.
While he's shielded, any single-target spell on him earns the caster a Black Mantle (big accuracy debuff). Use All-Enemies spells, or expect to fizzle next turn.
This is the key cheat: a fizzle makes him shove every positive charm and ward — his Tower Shield included — onto a random wizard. Fizzling on purpose is the cleanest way to take his shield off him.
